response.justification: The candidate is a broadly experienced, craftsmanship-oriented engineer with genuine language-tooling interest (D test runners, assertion libraries) and prior IDE development experience at National Instruments (LabWindows/CVI in C/C++). However, the role's core requirements are not met: there is no demonstrated Kotlin experience, only minor Java exposure, and no Spring or enterprise JVM framework background. His recent decade is dominated by TypeScript/Node.js and DLang work, making the JVM-ecosystem gap too large for a mid-senior IC role focused on Java/Kotlin/Spring tooling.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and authored developer tooling (test runner, assertion library)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, pairing, testable design, plus teaching/workshops
Proven track record on complex, high-scale systems (millions of requests/sec, low-latency services)
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only peripheral Java usage; JD requires strong expertise in both
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework experience
Recent work centers on TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, distant from the enterprise JVM ecosystem
